steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Rewrite the circle equation in center-radius form.
$$x^2-6x+y^2-12y-36=0$$
$$x^2-6x+y^2-12y=36$$
$$(x-3)^2+(y-6)^2=81$$

steps.title: Use the circle radius
steps.content: The radius is $9$, so the diameter is $18$. For a circle inscribed in a square, the square's side length equals the circle's diameter.

steps.title: Find the perimeter
steps.content:
The square has side length $18$, so its perimeter is $4$ times the side length.
$$4\cdot18=72$$
The answer is C.
